perturbation.original_text: All CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, which RECIPIENT shall use, view, receive or come in contact with, shall be and shall remain the COMPANY'S SOLE and EXCLUSIVE property, and shall be PROMPTLY RETURNED upon completion of the purpose for which it was provided, or when verbally communicated, will no longer be utilized to benefit RECIPIENT, but in NO EVENT, later than TEN (10) DAYS after request by COMPANY.
perturbation.changed_text: All CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, which RECIPIENT shall use, view, receive or come in contact with, shall be and shall remain the COMPANY'S SOLE and EXCLUSIVE property, and may be retained by RECIPIENT indefinitely after the business relationship ends, regardless of whether the purpose for which it was provided is complete.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a contradiction regarding the return of confidential material. Section 2 originally states that all confidential material must be promptly returned, but this modification states it may be retained indefinitely. This creates uncertainty about the RECIPIENT's obligations. The contradiction takes place specifically in the changed Section 2, directly contradicting the original text about the return of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, which RECIPIENT shall use, view, receive or come in contact with, shall be and shall remain the COMPANY'S SOLE and EXCLUSIVE property, and may be retained by RECIPIENT indefinitely after the business relationship ends, regardless of whether the purpose for which it was provided is complete. NO COPIES shall be made of ANY MATERIAL or DOCUMENT (S) provided under this agreement, except upon WRITTEN CONSENT of the COMPANY, ALL COPIES and samples shall likewise be RETURNED. Any attempt to disassemble or otherwise reverse engineer CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL by the RECIPIENT or the transfer of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to any other company or persons for any reason is strictly prohibited.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all confidential material shall be promptly returned. The changed text states that the confidential material may be retained indefinitely. The contradicted text contains both statements, creating an in-text contradiction because it's unclear whether the RECIPIENT should return the material or can retain it indefinitely. This directly contradicts the return obligation described in the original text, now resulting in conflicting possibilities, which aligns with the given explanation and the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: RECIPIENT agrees as follows: 1. RECIPIENT may view, have access to, and through verbal explanations learn of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, samples, and other information, financial information, marketing information, data, special testing and training procedures and processes, specifications either owned by the COMPANY or used in the course of its business (collectively called "CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL").
perturbation.changed_text: RECIPIENT agrees as follows: 1. RECIPIENT may view, have access to, and through verbal explanations learn of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, samples, and other information, financial information, marketing information, data, special testing and training procedures and processes, specifications either owned by the COMPANY or used in the course of its business (collectively called "CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL"), but is under no obligation to keep it confidential.
perturbation.explanation: The original contract defines Confidential Material and implicitly states that it is to be kept confidential by the RECIPIENT, as demonstrated in section 3. The altered version explicitly states the RECIPIENT is 'under no obligation to keep it confidential,' directly contradicting the intention of the entire agreement and creating a direct contradiction in Section 3 where it discusses the obligation to keep CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL secret. This creates uncertainty about the core purpose of the non-disclosure agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During and after discussion, including the duration of ANY BUSINESS RELATIONSHIP and THEREAFTER, RECIPIENT will HOLD TRUST and KEEP SECRET ALL CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL obtained from COMPANY. ---- RECIPIENT will maintain adequate SAFEGUARDS to PROTECT such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and PREVENT its DISCLOSURE to others, except its own employees on a NEED-TO-KNOW BASIS, and will NEITHER USE nor DISCLOSE, DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY, for him/herself or for the benefit of another, ANY CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL or perform any acts which may DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY have an adverse effect upon the business of the COMPANY, or which would tend to reduce the proprietary value of such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to the COMPANY, without the COMPANY'S PRIOR WRITTEN APPROVAL. RECIPIENT shall MAINTAIN a RECORD or LOG reflecting the NAMES, ADDRESSES, DATES and other INFORMATION which the COMPANY shall request RECIPIENT to keep of PERSONS or ENTITIES to execute a COPY of this AGREEMENT acknowledging its TERMS and the TRADE SECRET nature of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and shall provide to the COMPANY UPON REQUEST, copies of ALL such acknowledgments.
perturbation.justification: The original text implicitly states that the Confidential Material is to be kept secret. The changed text explicitly states that the RECIPIENT is under no obligation to keep it confidential. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the RECIPIENT will HOLD TRUST and KEEP SECRET ALL CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL obtained from the COMPANY. Therefore, there is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: NO COPIES shall be made of ANY MATERIAL or DOCUMENT (S) provided under this agreement, except upon WRITTEN CONSENT of the COMPANY, ALL COPIES and samples shall likewise be RETURNED.
perturbation.changed_text: NO COPIES shall be made of ANY MATERIAL or DOCUMENT (S) provided under this agreement, and copies of ANY MATERIAL or DOCUMENT(S) can be made, without the WRITTEN CONSENT of the COMPANY, ALL COPIES and samples shall likewise be RETURNED.
perturbation.explanation: The original text restricts copying without written consent, and copies are meant to be returned. The changed text allows copying 'without WRITTEN CONSENT', directly contradicting the first half of the sentence. This makes enforcement ambiguous. While the second part of the sentence talks about returning all COPIES and samples, the first part directly contradict the need for a WRITTEN CONSENT to make copies of ANY MATERIAL.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, which RECIPIENT shall use, view, receive or come in contact with, shall be and shall remain the COMPANY'S SOLE and EXCLUSIVE property, and shall be PROMPTLY RETURNED upon completion of the purpose for which it was provided, or when verbally communicated, will no longer be utilized to benefit RECIPIENT, but in NO EVENT, later than TEN (10) DAYS after request by COMPANY. NO COPIES shall be made of ANY MATERIAL or DOCUMENT (S) provided under this agreement, and copies of ANY MATERIAL or DOCUMENT(S) can be made, without the WRITTEN CONSENT of the COMPANY, ALL COPIES and samples shall likewise be RETURNED. Any attempt to disassemble or otherwise reverse engineer CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL by the RECIPIENT or the transfer of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to any other company or persons for any reason is strictly prohibited.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that copies can be made 'without the WRITTEN CONSENT of the COMPANY'. The contradicted text, which includes the changed text, initially states 'NO COPIES shall be made of ANY MATERIAL or DOCUMENT'. The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by allowing copies without written consent, which conflicts with the initial prohibition of making copies, thus meeting the definition of a structural flaw leading to in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any attempt to disassemble or otherwise reverse engineer CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL by the RECIPIENT or the transfer of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to any other company or persons for any reason is strictly prohibited.
perturbation.changed_text: Any attempt to disassemble or otherwise reverse engineer CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL by the RECIPIENT or the transfer of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to any other company or persons is permitted with explicit WRITTEN CONSENT by the COMPANY.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, any attempt to disassemble or reverse engineer CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL was strictly prohibited. But the modification makes it permitted with WRITTEN CONSENT, contradicting the prior statement. This creates uncertainty whether reverse engineering is generally prohibited. The RECIPIENT doesn't know what is permitted and not. This changes the definition in Section 2 directly.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, which RECIPIENT shall use, view, receive or come in contact with, shall be and shall remain the COMPANY'S SOLE and EXCLUSIVE property, and shall be PROMPTLY RETURNED upon completion of the purpose for which it was provided, or when verbally communicated, will no longer be utilized to benefit RECIPIENT, but in NO EVENT, later than TEN (10) DAYS after request by COMPANY. NO COPIES shall be made of ANY MATERIAL or DOCUMENT (S) provided under this agreement, except upon WRITTEN CONSENT of the COMPANY, ALL COPIES and samples shall likewise be RETURNED. Any attempt to disassemble or otherwise reverse engineer CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL by the RECIPIENT or the transfer of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to any other company or persons is permitted with explicit WRITTEN CONSENT by the COMPANY.
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibited reverse engineering of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL. The changed text permits it with written consent. The contradicted text includes the changed text which now says that 'Any attempt to disassemble or otherwise reverse engineer CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL by the RECIPIENT or the transfer of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to any other company or persons is permitted with explicit WRITTEN CONSENT by the COMPANY.' This directly contradicts the original intention of prohibiting reverse engineering, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The recipient is no longer clear on whether they are permitted or prohibited from reverse engineering the material.

perturbation.original_text: RECIPIENT shall MAINTAIN a RECORD or LOG reflecting the NAMES, ADDRESSES, DATES and other INFORMATION which the COMPANY shall request RECIPIENT to keep of PERSONS or ENTITIES to execute a COPY of this AGREEMENT acknowledging its TERMS and the TRADE SECRET nature of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and shall provide to the COMPANY UPON REQUEST, copies of ALL such acknowledgments.
perturbation.changed_text: RECIPIENT shall MAINTAIN a RECORD or LOG reflecting the NAMES, ADDRESSES, DATES and other INFORMATION which the COMPANY shall request RECIPIENT to keep of PERSONS or ENTITIES to execute a COPY of this AGREEMENT acknowledging its TERMS and the TRADE SECRET nature of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, however, RECIPIENT is not obligated to provide to the COMPANY UPON REQUEST, copies of ALL such acknowledgments.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that RECIPIENT must provide copies of acknowledgments to the COMPANY upon request. This change adds an exception stating that RECIPIENT is not obligated to provide these copies. This is a direct contradiction within Section 3, as it first requires keeping records and then disclaims the obligation to share them. It affects the company's ability to monitor compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During and after discussion, including the duration of ANY BUSINESS RELATIONSHIP and THEREAFTER, RECIPIENT will HOLD TRUST and KEEP SECRET ALL CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL obtained from COMPANY. ---- RECIPIENT will maintain adequate SAFEGUARDS to PROTECT such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and PREVENT its DISCLOSURE to others, except its own employees on a NEED-TO-KNOW BASIS, and will NEITHER USE nor DISCLOSE, DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY, for him/herself or for the benefit of another, ANY CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL or perform any acts which may DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY have an adverse effect upon the business of the COMPANY, or which would tend to reduce the proprietary value of such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to the COMPANY, without the COMPANY'S PRIOR WRITTEN APPROVAL. RECIPIENT shall MAINTAIN a RECORD or LOG reflecting the NAMES, ADDRESSES, DATES and other INFORMATION which the COMPANY shall request RECIPIENT to keep of PERSONS or ENTITIES to execute a COPY of this AGREEMENT acknowledging its TERMS and the TRADE SECRET nature of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, however, RECIPIENT is not obligated to provide to the COMPANY UPON REQUEST, copies of ALL such acknowledgments.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction. The original text requires the RECIPIENT to provide copies of acknowledgments upon request. The changed text explicitly states that the RECIPIENT is *not* obligated to provide these copies, despite still requiring the RECIPIENT to maintain a record of such acknowledgements. This creates a clear inconsistency in Section 3, as it first requires keeping records and then disclaims the obligation to share them which is what the contradicted text states. This aligns with the provided definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of ACTUAL VIOLATION of the AGREEMENT, the COMPANY shall receive, from RECIPIENT 100% of ALL INCOME which RECIPIENT has acquired form said BREACH of this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of ACTUAL VIOLATION of the AGREEMENT, the COMPANY may receive, at the court's discretion, a percentage (0-100%) of ALL INCOME which RECIPIENT has acquired form said BREACH of this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that 100% of income acquired from a breach goes to the COMPANY. The modified version introduces ambiguity by giving the court discretion to decide the percentage, which could be 0%. This contradicts the original certainty of the remedy, making it unclear what the COMPANY will receive in case of a breach. It inserts uncertainty in the consequences of breaching the agreement which appears in Section 6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any VIOLATION or THREATENED VIOLATION of this AGREEMENT shall entitle the COMPANY to INJUNCTIVE RELIEF, together with ANY OTHER REMEDIES available to the COMPANY including MONETARY DAMAGES. In the event of ACTUAL VIOLATION of the AGREEMENT, the COMPANY may receive, at the court's discretion, a percentage (0-100%) of ALL INCOME which RECIPIENT has acquired form said BREACH of this AGREEMENT. 7. If any provision in this AGREEMENT is held, by a COURT of competent jurisdiction, to be invalid, void or unenforceable, the remaining provision shall nevertheless CONTINUE in FULL FORCE, without being impaired or invalidated IN ANY WAY. The failure of the COMPANY to enforce any provision of this AGREEMENT shall NOT be construed as a waiver of any such provision, nor prevent the COMPANY from enforcing such provision or any other provision of this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the COMPANY 'shall receive, from RECIPIENT 100% of ALL INCOME,' which mandates a specific outcome. The changed text replaces 'shall' with 'may receive, at the court's discretion, a percentage (0-100%),' introducing uncertainty and the possibility of receiving nothing. This directly contradicts the original text's clear entitlement and creates a conflict with the contradicted text, which expects the COMPANY to have 'ANY OTHER REMEDIES available to the COMPANY including MONETARY DAMAGES,' implying a certain, rather than discretionary, remedy.
